brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is operator-algebraic and topological: projections in a von Neumann algebra with a trace serve as local quantum measurements from which a simplicial complex is built to read off spacetime topology. Paper B's machinery is birational-geometric and analytic: resolution of singularities reduces an interaction kernel's analytic singularity to a monomial model to characterize integrability (Morrey-exponent admissibility) and thus PDE well-posedness. There is no role-for-role correspondence between measurement projections/simplicial homology and resolution-theoretic vanishing orders/log-canonical thresholds, so the overlap is at most the generic word 'geometry/topology', not shared machinery.
